name: Component
summary: The <component> element — defines a brand-new custom element entirely from HTML (tag name, template, shadow options). The fourth gap in Web Components, alongside scoped registries, injector integration, and transient components. Intended implementation: a deterministic build-time AST transform to a class-based element.

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom elements registered via customElements.define()
customElements.adoption: The <component> definition lowers to an autonomous element class + customElements.define(). Autonomous only (Safari refuses customized built-ins).

declarativeShadowDOM.pattern: <template shadowrootmode> attaches a shadow root during HTML parsing, no JS
declarativeShadowDOM.adoption: shadow="open|closed" maps onto attachShadow / DSD. The component's children become the element's shadow content. attachShadow options forward as boolean attributes — delegates-focus maps onto attachShadow({ delegatesFocus: true }) / shadowrootdelegatesfocus.

htmlTemplate.adoption: The component's own children are the render source (implicit template, DC-11) — cloned into the shadow (or light) tree on connect. A lone direct-child <template> is the explicit, inert form for un-built runtime delivery or parser-sensitive markup; both lower identically.

slot.pattern: <slot> projects light-DOM children into shadow content
slot.adoption: Content projection is native — the definition uses <slot> directly; no custom projection layer.

scopedRegistries.adoption: Tier-2 scope attribute composes with the project's Scoped Registries section; global customElements by default.

declarativeCustomElements.pattern: <definition name constructor> — define a custom element from markup
declarativeCustomElements.adoption: Prior art. We choose <component> over <definition>; we ship the static tier now and compose the unshipped binding primitives (Template Instantiation, DOM Parts) later.

elementInternals.pattern: attachInternals() exposes form participation (static formAssociated), default ARIA semantics (internals.role), and custom states (internals.states / :state())
elementInternals.adoption: form-associated maps onto static formAssociated + attachInternals(); default-role maps onto internals.role default semantics. Custom states and form value/validation wiring are deferred (DC-14, compose with Web Validation).

atomicMove.pattern: Element.moveBefore() relocates a connected node without disconnecting it; a connectedMoveCallback opts custom elements into state preservation
atomicMove.adoption: preserve-on-move emits an empty connectedMoveCallback() so moveBefore() preserves the element's state (focus, media, iframes, shadow) instead of teardown/rebuild.

openForProgressiveResolution.description: The design forks below are tracked as open points (DC-1..DC-15) in reports/2026-06-03-declarative-component-element.md and as backlog 'decision' threads. They carry a current recommendation, not a final ruling.
openForProgressiveResolution.rationale: The platform is landing declarative-component primitives bottom-up; resolving these by fiat now would couple the standard to unsettled upstream shapes. Each resolves independently as evidence arrives.

tagName.description: The definition element is <component name="...">. (Open point DC-1.)
tagName.rationale: Reads naturally as the thing you author. The name attribute + the define-here children disambiguate it from Vue's instantiate-here <component :is>. Alternatives kept open: <define-element>, <custom-element>, the WICG <definition>.

shadowAttribute.description: shadow="open|closed|none" selects the shadow mode; none means a light-DOM component. The native DSD spelling shadowrootmode="open|closed" is a recognized alias (1:1 with the open|closed sub-axis); shadow= is the default because only it adds the light-DOM 'none' case DSD has no word for. (Open point DC-2.)
shadowAttribute.rationale: Shorter than native shadowrootmode and adds the light-DOM case DSD has no word for, so shadow= is the default — but per most-flexible-default + the #046 precedent (default tag + recognized alias), the native shadowrootmode spelling is an accepted alias, not withheld: shadowrootmode="open|closed" desugars identically to shadow="open|closed". Authoring vocabulary only; no behavior change (#045/#615). Documented as mapping onto attachShadow / Declarative Shadow DOM.

registrationTiming.description: <component> is an autonomous element that registers its definition in connectedCallback, then removes itself from the DOM. (Open point DC-3.)
registrationTiming.rationale: Reuses the transient-component self-removal lifecycle (queueMicrotask + remove), so definitions can sit in <head> or anywhere in <body> with no global parse-time scanner and no residual wrapper node.

reactiveDepth.description: Tier 1 is static: clone template, project slots, attach shadow. Bindings ({{expr}}), if/each compose later. (Open point DC-4.)
reactiveDepth.rationale: Matches the WG's bottom-up strategy — reactive templating composes with Web Expressions / for-each / Template Instantiation as those mature, rather than baking an unshipped binding syntax into the standard now.

scriptingHook.description: Declarative-only in tier 1. A tier-2 behavior/extends hook associates a registered class/trait for progressive enhancement. No inline <script> in the definition. DC-5 ratified this POLICY (2026-06-08); the resolution MECHANISM (how a registry-key resolves+applies without breaching the wc-class self-contained-ESM/fixed-member-order invariants) is the open decision #852. (Open point DC-5 → build decision #852.)
scriptingHook.rationale: Answers WICG #1009's 'can a declarative element be enhanced with script?' without the script-gadget XSS surface that an inline <script> in a parsed definition would open.

implicitTemplate.description: The component's own children are the template — no nested <template> required. A lone direct-child <template> is the explicit, inert escape hatch; both forms lower to byte-identical output. A childless <component> is an empty template. (Open point DC-11; supersedes the narrower DC-9 'omitted = empty' framing.)
implicitTemplate.rationale: Children-as-template is the nicer authoring surface and is safe under the intended build-time AST transform — it reads source text, so children never go live in a browser. The inert <template> wrapper is kept for un-built runtime delivery and for parser-sensitive content (e.g. table-fragment <tr> the parser drops outside a table, leak-prone <style> before self-removal). The remaining open edge is whether to ever require the explicit form.

shadowInitOptions.description: attachShadow() options forward as boolean attributes that map onto the matching Declarative Shadow DOM attribute: delegates-focus (delegatesFocus / shadowrootdelegatesfocus), clonable (shadowrootclonable), and serializable (shadowrootserializable) are all implemented. Only valid with a shadow root (shadow != none). Tracked in backlog 076.
shadowInitOptions.rationale: Map-through, native-first: the platform already settled the spelling via DSD, so we forward it into the lowering's attachShadow(init) rather than invent new vocabulary. Option keys emit in a fixed order to keep generated output byte-identical.

elementInternals.description: attachInternals() is exposed declaratively: form-associated (DC-12) emits static formAssociated + attachInternals() for form participation; default-role="…" (DC-13) sets internals.role default ARIA semantics (instance role= still overrides); default-aria-* (#853) extends DC-13 to the rest of the string-valued internals.aria* surface (e.g. ariaLabel/ariaChecked/ariaHasPopup/ariaValueNow) via the same constructor-time map-through, with unknown keys rejected. Custom states / :state() (DC-14) and form value/validation wiring are deferred (tier-2/3, compose with Web Validation). Tracked in backlog 082.
elementInternals.rationale: ElementInternals is the platform's imperative-only surface with no HTML form today, so it is the richest declarative target. default-role is chosen over reusing role= to avoid ambiguity on the definition element (the definition's own role vs the defined element's default). The generated class sets internals.role in the constructor and declares static formAssociated; the runtime twin guards attachInternals for non-browser test runtimes (happy-dom) while real browsers wire it up.

preserveOnMove.description: preserve-on-move (boolean) emits an empty connectedMoveCallback(). Defining the callback opts the element into atomic moves: Element.moveBefore() preserves state (focus, media, iframes, shadow tree) instead of firing disconnected + connected. (Open point DC-15.) Tracked in backlog 084.
preserveOnMove.rationale: The opt-in IS the method's presence, so an empty body suffices and the lowering stays declarative-complete (no author JS). The method is conditionally added so other elements keep default disconnect/reconnect semantics. happy-dom lacks moveBefore, so unit tests assert source emission + conditional prototype presence; real browsers exercise the move.

intendedImplementation.description: Reference implementation is a deterministic, build-time, AST-based transform to a class element — the declarative-component adapter — not a browser-side interpreter.
intendedImplementation.rationale: Deterministic (byte-identical output for identical input, stable member order), AST-based (parse then emit, never regex), zero residual runtime for tier 1 (baseline DOM APIs only). Same lowering Svelte/Lit-style tooling performs.

vue.notes: Vue's <component :is> is the instantiate-here pattern our define-here element must not be confused with (DC-1).

svelte.notes: Compiles components to classes at build time — the same model as our AST-transform adapter.

lit.notes: The class shape our transform emits resembles a Lit element's render/connectedCallback.

stampino.notes: Userland proof that HTML-defined components are viable today; informs the static tier.
